title: Andhra Pulihora (Tamarind Rice)

ingredients.name: cooked rice, cooled (ponni or sona masoori)

subs.name: cashews for a richer, nut-allergy-safe version

ingredients.name: gingelly oil
visual_checks: sesame oil is traditional; adds characteristic flavour
ingredients.notes: sesame oil is traditional; adds characteristic flavour

subs.name: regular cooking oil; less aromatic but acceptable

steps.text: Soak the tamarind in 200 ml hot water for 10 minutes, then squeeze and strain to extract a thick pulp. Discard the fibres.
Heat gingelly oil in a kadai over medium flame. Add mustard seeds; when they pop, add chana dal and urad dal. Fry until the dals turn golden, about 1 minute.
Add dried red chillies, green chillies and curry leaves; fry for 30 seconds. Add asafoetida and turmeric, then pour in the tamarind extract. Add jaggery and salt.

Boil the tamarind mixture over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally, until it thickens to a concentrate and the raw tamarind smell transforms into a mellow sour aroma — about 8–10 minutes. The paste should coat the back of a spoon.
Remove from heat and stir in the roasted peanuts. Pour this tamarind tempering over the cooled rice.

Mix gently but thoroughly with clean hands or a flat spoon so every grain is coated and the rice turns a uniform golden-yellow. Taste and adjust salt and tamarind. Serve at room temperature — pulihora improves as it sits for 30 minutes.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: gingelly-oil → regular cooking oil; less aromatic but acceptable; peanut → cashews for a richer, nut-allergy-safe version
